_readme: Local generation catalog. Focus weights select templates; each template declares its reasoning mode and evidence bundle.

strategies.name: Explain
strategies.prefix: Explain a concrete purpose, responsibility, or design rationale supported by the evidence.

strategies.name: Execution
strategies.prefix: Mentally execute the supplied code or workflow evidence step by step.

strategies.name: Prediction
strategies.prefix: Predict an outcome for a short scenario that is fully supported by the evidence.

strategies.name: Debugging
strategies.prefix: Identify a concrete fault or risky behavior and the correction that addresses it.

strategies.name: Modification
strategies.prefix: Choose a change that satisfies a new requirement while preserving relevant behavior.

evidence_types.name: Module Graph
evidence_types.description: Source modules and their high-level organisation.

evidence_types.name: Data Flow
evidence_types.description: Functions and paths that move or transform data.

evidence_types.name: Control Flow
evidence_types.description: Functions and paths containing branches, loops, and state changes.

evidence_types.name: Symbol Table
evidence_types.description: Declared classes, functions, methods, and module-level symbols.

evidence_types.name: Complexity Analysis
evidence_types.description: Heuristic function-size, loop, branch, and call indicators.

evidence_types.name: Test Discovery
evidence_types.description: Discovered test files and test-like functions.

evidence_types.name: API Discovery
evidence_types.description: Routes, endpoints, requests, responses, and external communication.

evidence_types.name: SQL Analysis
evidence_types.description: SQL files, statements, schemas, and persistence operations.

templates.name: Purpose / Responsibility
templates.pattern: Name a concrete component or pipeline stage in the stem, then test a specific input/output behavior, interaction, side effect, condition, or consequence that is not obvious from its identifier or docstring. Never ask the broad purpose of a descriptively named function, never ask the student to select the responsible function/class/module by name, and never use bare identifiers as the options. Do not ask library trivia.
templates.query: purpose responsibility role operation function class component stage

required.label: concrete implementation
required.query: function class method process transform publish load save

templates.name: Workflow
templates.pattern: Ask how data or control moves through concrete project stages in a valid order, including the resulting output or side effect.
templates.query: workflow order pipeline input process transform output call flow

required.label: execution or data path
required.query: entry run pipeline call flow read transform publish save

templates.name: Design Behavior
templates.pattern: Name relevant components in the stem, then ask about a non-obvious evidenced relationship between at least two components: a boundary, dependency, shared downstream path, separation, ordering, or cross-component consequence that cannot be guessed from an identifier or docstring. Never ask what happens when one named function is called. Every option must be a complete relational claim; never ask the broad purpose/behavior of a descriptively named function, never ask which function/class/module is responsible, and never offer bare identifiers as options. Wrong options must assert relationships contradicted by evidence. Never ask which design is best or infer undocumented advantages.
templates.query: architecture design boundary dependency abstraction separation behavior relationship

templates.name: Scenario / Edge Case
templates.pattern: Show the complete minimal code branch for a concrete condition, failure, state change, or side effect. Use a neutral stem such as 'For the shown code, if <condition>, which observable behavior occurs?' and do not presuppose that a print, update, error, or return happens. The student must be able to derive every option from the displayed code without remembering the project. The correct answer must state the executed calls, assignments, return, or output exactly; never call a visible print or update 'no action' or 'nothing happens'. Avoid unsupported hypothetical features.
templates.query: scenario failure empty invalid boundary exception retry state outcome

required.label: conditional behavior
required.query: if else except error retry empty missing invalid

templates.name: Code Explain
templates.pattern: Show one minimal self-contained code excerpt and ask its concrete purpose. The correct option must cover all material visible effects, including conditional calls, rather than describing only the first assignment.
templates.query: function method implementation purpose code behavior

templates.name: Code Trace / Outcome
templates.pattern: Show a minimal self-contained excerpt and ask about its output, state change, return value, or call order under a stated input.
templates.query: trace output return state variable call order behavior

templates.name: Debug / Correct
templates.pattern: Show a self-contained excerpt with one demonstrable fault, or a clearly identified proposed change containing the fault, and ask for the correction. Do not falsely claim the original project is broken.
templates.query: debug error bug incorrect exception boundary condition fix

required.label: code with relevant control flow
required.query: if else loop try except return update validate

templates.name: Modification / Insertion
templates.pattern: State one new requirement, show sufficient insertion or replacement context, and ask which short change belongs there while preserving current behavior.
templates.query: modify insert requirement refactor extend change implementation

templates.name: Schema / Integrity
templates.pattern: Ask about an evidenced schema, field relationship, constraint, persistence operation, or data-integrity rule.
templates.query: schema table column key constraint relation database sql integrity model

templates.name: Test / Expected Behavior
templates.pattern: Ask which test, assertion, or boundary case verifies a concrete behavior shown by the implementation.
templates.query: test assertion expected behavior boundary fixture failure validation

topics.name: Architecture
topics.description: Internal components, boundaries, dependencies, and how project parts work together.
topics.query: architecture module package dependency layer component boundary structure

topics.name: Integration / API
topics.description: External APIs, services, databases, message queues, contracts, payloads, failures, and retries.
topics.query: integration api endpoint request response client payload mqtt publish subscribe external service retry

topics.name: Workflow / Data Flow
topics.description: How control or data moves from inputs through processing stages to outputs.
topics.query: workflow data flow pipeline input output transform validate load save process stage

topics.name: Implementation / Code Logic
topics.description: Local rules, transformations, conditions, state changes, and implementation behavior.
topics.query: implementation code logic function condition state behavior transformation calculation

topics.name: Data Modelling
topics.description: Persistence, schemas, relationships, queries, constraints, and data integrity.
topics.query: database schema table query sql transaction persist model record relationship constraint

topics.name: Security
topics.description: Validation, authentication, authorization, secrets, and safe data handling.
topics.query: security authenticate authorize permission input validation secret token encrypt sanitize

topics.name: Testing
topics.description: Test cases, assertions, expected behavior, boundaries, and fault detection.
topics.query: test testing assertion fixture validate expected error boundary behavior

topics.name: Complexity
topics.description: Time, space, scalability, resource use, and performance trade-offs.
topics.query: complexity performance efficient optimize scalability time space memory loop

topics.name: OOP
topics.description: Classes, objects, encapsulation, inheritance, composition, and collaboration.
topics.query: oop object class method inheritance composition interface encapsulation polymorphism
